summary: I was born and raised in South Korea, specifically in a city called Seongnam near Seoul. I lived there for 19 years before moving abroad. South Korea is where my roots are, and leaving it was both exciting and a bit sad as I embarked on my journey to study in Japan and later the United States.

Japan became a significant part of my life when I decided to attend a university in Tokyo. I first visited Tokyo at 16 and was captivated by its beauty and calm atmosphere, which inspired me to study there. I moved to Japan two years later, faced challenges adapting to the culture and language, but eventually settled until the COVID-19 pandemic forced me to return to Korea.

In August 2021, I arrived at the University of Minnesota as an exchange student from Japan. It was my first time in the United States, and I had to adjust to new social customs like small talk, which was unfamiliar to me from Korea and Japan. My time at UMN has broadened my perspective, and I am grateful for the meaningful experiences I gained there despite the challenges of the pandemic.

Seongnam is the city where I was born and spent my childhood, located close to Seoul. It is a planned city with very organized buildings and roads, which made the environment feel uniform and a bit boring to me. Growing up there, I always dreamed of exploring other cultures and places beyond Seongnam’s structured neighborhoods.

Seoul is the major city near where I grew up, in Seongnam. Although I didn't live directly in Seoul, its proximity was part of my early life experience in South Korea. My move from this region marked the beginning of my international journey to Japan and later the United States.

Tokyo first captured my heart when I visited at age 16, with its stunning night views and peaceful atmosphere. This visit inspired me to pursue higher education there, and two years later I moved to Tokyo to attend university. Living in Tokyo was challenging at first, especially adapting to the language and culture, but it became my home until the pandemic disrupted my studies.

The COVID-19 pandemic greatly affected my life as a student abroad. When it hit, all my classes in Japan shifted online, and I had to return to Korea for a year and a half, which felt like a lost time. However, it also motivated me to apply for the exchange program to the United States once it resumed, allowing me to continue my international education.

The United States became my next destination when I joined the University of Minnesota as an exchange student in 2021. It was a new cultural experience, especially learning about social customs like small talk, which were different from Korea and Japan. My time in the US has expanded my worldview and enriched my journey as an international student.
